caveats: a: No occupation source isolates NAICS 921130, so the broad-government mix is only a proxy.
a: The assigned labor-share input is missing because government establishments have no comparable receipts denominator.
a: Exposure describes public-agency work despite the fixed commercial lens having no eligible firms.
a: Existing rules engines, electronic filing, and analytics must not be counted again as new AI opportunity.
rho: The estimate applies only to the exposed opportunity in a and not to total agency labor.
rho: Federal evidence may not transfer to smaller state and local finance offices with different systems and capabilities.
rho: An active use-case inventory does not establish scaled deployment or realized labor substitution.
rho: Regulated decisions often require human review even when AI produces an initial recommendation.
e: The assigned LMM firm-count input is missing, consistent with government and SUSB scope exclusion.
e: Zero is a structural lens conclusion, not an imputation for missing firm data.
e: Government contractors may be economically relevant but are outside this NAICS code.
s5: Public-sector reorganizations are not qualifying control transfers.
s5: No commercial transaction denominator exists for this lens.
q: Budgetary capacity and increased tax collection are not equivalent to commercial retained gross benefit.
q: Appropriation rules may remove, redeploy, or supplement savings in ways the q construct does not represent.
d5: BLS output aggregates are far broader than public finance and use national production concepts rather than administrative workload.
d5: CBO projections cover federal finances only and fiscal dollars are not equivalent to constant-quality service quantity.
d5: Legislation and administrative restructuring can change workload abruptly.

o: Private vendors may supply technology and operations while legal accountability remains with government.
o: This describes public-function durability, not an investable private operator population.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: The classified establishments exercise statutory public authority and are funded through appropriations, taxes, fees, or public funds. They do not earn recurring outsourced-service revenue from external commercial customers, and their budgets, public benefits, and legal mandates are not transferable EBITDA.
lens.deviation_from_default: The default lens produces an empty population because Census defines this industry as government establishments. There is no coherent narrowing that converts a public-finance agency into a transferable private firm without leaving NAICS 921130.
lens.excluded_activities: All government departments, agencies, authorities, treasuries, tax offices, central-bank functions, public retirement administrations, and other public establishments are excluded from the transferable-firm population because they are not privately controlled commercial firms; private contractors are classified according to the service they provide rather than as NAICS 921130.
lens.included_activities: No privately owned firm satisfies the fixed lens. For operational context only, the classified activities are government public-finance functions including tax administration and collection, monetary policy, custody and disbursement of public funds, debt and investment administration, government auditing, and public employee retirement trust administration.

a.bridge: BLS measures occupations across all federal, state, and local government rather than NAICS 921130, while Census identifies this industry's work as finance, tax, audit, debt, investment, fund-custody, and monetary-policy administration. I raise the broad-government information-work share because these functions are unusually document-, rule-, data-, analysis-, and transaction-intensive, then reserve substantial human work for judgment, investigation, authorization, stakeholder interaction, and accountability. The proxy could overstate substitution where statutory discretion dominates or understate it in high-volume clerical processing.
caveats: No occupation source isolates NAICS 921130, so the broad-government mix is only a proxy.
The assigned labor-share input is missing because government establishments have no comparable receipts denominator.
Exposure describes public-agency work despite the fixed commercial lens having no eligible firms.
Existing rules engines, electronic filing, and analytics must not be counted again as new AI opportunity.
a.change_evidence: A wage-weighted task inventory for federal, state, and local NAICS 921130 establishments that separates already automated rules and analytics from remaining tasks would materially change the range.
a.rationale: Broad government employment includes 13.86 percent office and administrative support and 6.51 percent business and financial operations, but public finance should be much more concentrated in analysts, accountants, auditors, budget staff, tax examiners, financial specialists, records, and transaction processing. AI can assist document intake, classification, reconciliation, anomaly and fraud detection, forecasting, research, taxpayer support, drafting, and audit selection. Final legal determinations, monetary and fiscal policy, enforcement, fund authorization, and public accountability remain human responsibilities.

d5.bridge: BLS projects real output for federal government and for state and local government excluding education and hospitals, not public finance specifically. Their 2024-to-2034 annual output growth rates of 0.4 percent and 0.6 percent imply five-year ratios near 1.02 and 1.03. I center on that range and widen it because CBO projects continued growth in federal revenues, deficits, debt, and interest costs, all of which sustain finance-administration complexity. The proxy may overstate public-finance growth if automation reduces transaction work or understate it if fiscal complexity and enforcement expand.
caveats: BLS output aggregates are far broader than public finance and use national production concepts rather than administrative workload.
CBO projections cover federal finances only and fiscal dollars are not equivalent to constant-quality service quantity.
Legislation and administrative restructuring can change workload abruptly.
d5.change_evidence: A constant-quality workload index for NAICS 921130 covering returns and payments processed, accounts administered, audits and appeals, debt issues, fund transactions, and public retirement accounts across all government levels would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Tax collection, custody and disbursement, budgeting, debt, investment, audit, retirement administration, and monetary-policy functions persist under law. Broader real government output is projected to grow slowly, while federal fiscal flows and debt remain large and increase over the coming decade. Policy change, staffing reductions, simplification, automation, and jurisdiction-specific finances create uncertainty around service quantity.

caveats: The assigned LMM firm-count input is missing, consistent with government and SUSB scope exclusion.
Zero is a structural lens conclusion, not an imputation for missing firm data.
Government contractors may be economically relevant but are outside this NAICS code.
e.change_evidence: A Census classification revision that explicitly admits privately controlled commercial establishments into NAICS 921130 would be required to move this value.
e.rationale: Census defines the industry as government establishments. A government finance agency is not a privately owned LMM firm supplying an outsourced commercial service, and a private contractor performing adjacent accounting, software, consulting, collections, or administrative work would be classified in its applicable private-service industry rather than NAICS 921130.

l.detail: No receipts concept for this code (government / SUSB out of scope). QCEW government wages exist ($26,081,053,760) but there is no revenue denominator; labor_share left null.
l.rationale: dataset labor_share has no defensible value

n.detail: margin 0.0000 (margins.json[92]: ESTIMATE: public administration — EBITDA is not meaningful for government units; margin 0 makes the n_band chain yield 0 by construction (recorded as a known gap)); revenue/employee $374,583 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at --); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: A high operator-required share does not imply current staffing or workflows remain intact.
Private vendors may supply technology and operations while legal accountability remains with government.
This describes public-function durability, not an investable private operator population.
o.change_evidence: Statutory changes transferring complete public-finance functions and accountability away from government establishments, or evidence that software legally replaces the accountable agency rather than assisting it, would materially lower the estimate.
o.rationale: Even with extensive automation, statutes assign taxation, public-fund custody, debt issuance, audit, retirement-trust administration, and monetary-policy authority to accountable government bodies and officials. Software can execute or recommend tasks but cannot itself hold sovereign authority, answer to courts and legislatures, authorize public funds, or bear public accountability. Some processing can be outsourced, yet the classified government operator remains responsible.

caveats: Budgetary capacity and increased tax collection are not equivalent to commercial retained gross benefit.
Appropriation rules may remove, redeploy, or supplement savings in ways the q construct does not represent.
q.change_evidence: A defined private operator population with observable customer contracts, repricing, and retained margins would be required to estimate this primitive.
q.rationale: Commercial benefit retention after customer pass-through or repricing is undefined. Public-finance establishments do not charge arm's-length outsourced-service prices or distribute EBITDA to private owners; efficiency may instead change budgets, service levels, backlogs, collections, oversight, or public value.

caveats: The estimate applies only to the exposed opportunity in a and not to total agency labor.
Federal evidence may not transfer to smaller state and local finance offices with different systems and capabilities.
An active use-case inventory does not establish scaled deployment or realized labor substitution.
Regulated decisions often require human review even when AI produces an initial recommendation.
rho.change_evidence: Audited agency-level benefit-realization reports for tax, treasury, audit, debt, investment, and retirement administration showing adoption, accuracy, appeals, control failures, staff hours avoided, and sustained production use would materially change the estimate.
rho.rationale: High-volume intake, research, drafting, reconciliation, taxpayer assistance, and decision support can be implemented within five years. IRS already reported numerous active AI use cases, but GAO identified incomplete inventories, skills gaps, information-quality weaknesses, and strategic-management problems. Privacy, cybersecurity, procurement, legacy systems, due process, explainability, model risk, records law, union and workforce constraints, and the need for accountable officials slow implementation.

caveats: Public-sector reorganizations are not qualifying control transfers.
No commercial transaction denominator exists for this lens.
s5.change_evidence: This primitive would become estimable only if the industry definition or lens admitted privately controlled commercial firms and a transaction population existed.
s5.rationale: A qualifying private control-transfer probability is undefined because the lens contains no eligible firms. Elections, appointments, reorganizations, jurisdictional consolidations, and statutory transfers of authority are not acquisitions of transferable commercial operating companies.

principal_driver: Statutory fiscal responsibilities and growing transaction and debt complexity sustain information-intensive work that AI can assist.
principal_weakness: The classification contains government establishments rather than transferable commercial firms, making firm availability and commercial value capture absent or undefined.

sources.supports: Government-only industry boundary and included public-finance functions: taxation, monetary policy, fund custody and disbursement, debt and investment administration, auditing, and public retirement trust administration.
sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 921130 Public Finance Activities

sources.supports: Broad federal, state, and local government occupation and wage mix, including office, business and financial, computer, budget, tax, accounting, audit, and financial-examiner roles; used only as a proxy.
sources.title: May 2023 Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates by Government Ownership

sources.supports: IRS AI uses including audit selection and taxpayer questions, its 126 active use cases as of June 2025, and implementation weaknesses involving skills, inventories, information quality, and management.
sources.title: Artificial Intelligence: IRS Actions Needed to Address Skills Gaps, Information Quality, and Strategic Management

sources.supports: Federal financial regulators' use and oversight of AI, potential efficiency benefits, and model, bias, data-quality, privacy, and cybersecurity risks; staff generally retain decision authority.
sources.title: Artificial Intelligence: Use and Oversight in Financial Services

sources.supports: Broader real-government output projections: 0.4 percent annual growth for federal government and 0.6 percent for state and local government excluding education and hospitals from 2024 to 2034.
sources.title: Industry employment and output projections to 2034

sources.supports: Projected federal revenues, outlays, deficits, debt, and interest costs, supporting continuing fiscal-administration scale and complexity rather than a direct service-demand measure.
sources.title: The Budget and Economic Outlook: 2026 to 2036

sources.supports: Treasury shared-services exploration and controlled testing of AI use cases for operational efficiency, indicating implementation activity but not yet measured scaled labor substitution.
sources.title: Administrative Resource Center Monthly Bulletin: August 2025

title: Public Finance Activities
